message.content:
You are an adversarial refuter. ASSUME this finding is FALSE and try to prove it. Read the actual code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay and find the code path / contract / test that makes the PoC impossible or the concern moot. Only conclude survives=true if you genuinely cannot disprove it.

Finding P-5: Unkeyed-trail warning and verify_trail=False fast path have no negative/behavioral test
File: src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:300
Description: Test-quality gap. `run_study` emits a `warnings.warn(...)` when the provenance trail is unkeyed (secret=None, line 300-305) and supports `verify_trail=False` to skip the O(N) re-hash (line 702-710). I find no test asserting (a) that the unkeyed warning actually fires on the default path (so a future refactor silencing it would go unnoticed — the warning is the only signal an operator gets that the trail is forgery-vulnerable), nor (b) that `verify_trail=False` actually skips `trail.verify()` while still producing a valid result, nor (c) that passing both `secret` and `trail` raises the documented ValueError (line 291-292). These are error/edge paths whose absence means the safety affordances can silently regress.
PoC: # grep of tests/ shows no assertion of the 'UNKEYED' warning, no test toggling verify_trail,
# and no test for the 'pass either a caller-owned trail OR a secret' ValueError guard.

Return survives=false if you can refute it (with the specific reason), survives=true if it withstands a genuine disproof attempt.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

280	    to an experiment store, best-effort (a tracker failure never aborts the run). ``versioner``
281	    (optional) content-addresses the source so the trail cites retrievable bytes. ``clock``
282	    injects a deterministic clock for the provenance timestamps only (ordering checks always use
283	    real time). ``secret`` keys the internal provenance trail with HMAC (forgery-resistant);
284	    mutually exclusive with passing your own ``trail``. ``trail`` may be a caller-owned
285	    :class:`ProvenanceTrail` so a run that *raises* still leaves the caller the partial trail.
286	
287	    Raises ``IngestionError`` on a bad source, ``FirewallViolation``/``PreRegistrationError`` on
288	    a methodological breach, ``GateError`` if a gate blocks, and ``ValueError`` on a malformed run.
289	    """
290	    tracer = tracer or OtelTracer()
291	    if trail is not None and secret is not None:
292	        raise ValueError("pass either a caller-owned trail OR a secret to key a new one, not both")
293	    if secret is None and trail is None:
294	        # An engine-created unkeyed trail is tamper-evident (a naive edit breaks the SHA-256
295	        # chain) but NOT forgery-resistant: an adversary with write access can edit an entry and
296	        # recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain. That is fine for local reproducibility but
297	        # insufficient for an audit trail that crosses a trust boundary. Flag the insecure
298	        # default rather than letting it pass silently (pass 3, #F-049); pass a `secret` to key
299	        # the trail with HMAC, or `clock=`-driven deterministic repro if forgery is out of scope.
300	        warnings.warn(
301	            "run_study provenance trail is UNKEYED (secret=None) — tamper-evident but not "
302	            "forgery-resistant. Pass secret=<bytes> to HMAC-key the trail for audit trails that "
303	            "cross a trust boundary.",
304	            stacklevel=2,
305	        )
306	    defn = plan.definition
307	    modes = defn.modes
308	    required = required_phases(modes)
309	    trail = trail if trail is not None else ProvenanceTrail(secret=secret, clock=clock)
310	    visited: list[Phase] = []
311	    run_id: str | None = None
312	
313	    def enter(phase: Phase, **payload: Any) -> None:
314	        visited.append(phase)
315	        trail.record("phase", f"enter {phase.name}", phase=phase.name, **payload)
316	
317	    def track(fn: Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]) -> None:
318	        # observability is best-effort: a tracker failure is recorded but never aborts a run.
319	        if tracker is None or run_id is None:
320	            return
321	        try:
322	            fn(tracker, run_id)
323	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — tracker is an external, optional backend
324	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker call failed: {exc}")
325	
326	    def run_gate(review: GateReview) -> None:
327	        # Record the decision once (snapshot approved/reason) and block on rejection (#94).
328	        decision = gate_handler(review)
329	        approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))

691	def test_verify_trail_can_be_opted_out(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
692	    # #F-036: end-of-run re-verification is O(N) and redundant for an in-memory trail the run

694	    # ProvenanceTrail.verify and assert it is NOT called when verify_trail=False (a no-op opt-out
695	    # that still verified would pass a chain-validity-only check, so we pin the SKIP directly).
696	    from assay_engine.provenance import ProvenanceTrail
697

700

702	        calls["n"] += 1
703	        return orig(self)

content.text: The finding is fully refuted. All referenced helpers and methods exist.

input.reasoning:
REFUTED. All three test gaps the finding claims are missing actually exist in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/test_pipeline.py and exercise exactly the affordances cited:

(a) UNKEYED warning fires on default path — `test_run_study_warns_when_trail_is_unkeyed` (line 679-688) runs run_study with no secret/trail under `warnings.simplefilter("always")` and asserts `pytest.warns(UserWarning, match="UNKEYED")`, directly pinning the line 300-305 warning.

(b) verify_trail=False skips the re-hash but still yields a valid result — `test_verify_trail_can_be_opted_out` (line 691-713) monkeypatches a counting spy onto `ProvenanceTrail.verify`, calls run_study with `verify_trail=False`, asserts the spy was NOT called (`calls["n"] == 0`), and then independently `verify_records(res.provenance)` confirms the produced chain is still intact. There is also a complementary positive test `test_verify_trail_default_still_verifies` (line 716-729) asserting the default invokes verify exactly once.

(c) secret+trail raises the documented ValueError — `test_secret_and_trail_are_mutually_exclusive` (line 747-754) asserts `pytest.raises(ValueError, match="either a caller-owned trail OR a secret")`, matching the guard at pipeline.py:291-292.

The finding's PoC ("grep of tests/ shows no assertion...") is factually wrong: grep over tests/ returns hits for UNKEYED, verify_trail (toggled both ways), and the mutual-exclusion ValueError. All test helpers referenced (auto_approve, verify_records, ProvenanceTrail, from_records) are imported/defined and the spied `verify()` method exists at provenance.py:202. Could not run pytest (no pytest module in this environment), but the test source is unambiguous and self-consistent. The safety affordances are covered; the test-quality gap does not exist.

content.text: Refuted. The finding is false. All three claimed-missing tests exist in `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/test_pipeline.py`: the UNKEYED warning assertion (line 679), the `verify_trail=False` opt-out spy test plus its default-on counterpart (lines 691, 716), and the secret+trail mutual-exclusion ValueError test (line 747).
